title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A shepherd's journey across the desert in search of treasure becomes a story about destiny and purpose.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches programmers to write readable, maintainable code.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An exploration of cosmology, black holes, and the origins of the universe for general readers.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: A woman discovers a library between life and death where each book holds a different version of her life.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas
description: From journeyman to master — essential wisdom on software craftsmanship and professional development.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping history of humankind from ancient hominids to the modern age through biology and culture.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: First published in 1925, this quintessential novel of the Jazz Age follows the mysteriously wealthy Jay Gatsby and his obsessive love for the beautiful Daisy Buchanan, amid lavish parties on Long Island — an exquisitely crafted tale of ambition, illusion, and the corruption of the American Dream.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of JavaScript, from types and scopes to async patterns.

title: The Gene
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: An intimate history of the gene, exploring the science and ethics of genetics from Mendel to CRISPR.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: A memoir about a woman who grows up in a survivalist family and eventually earns a PhD from Cambridge.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: A guide to the principles and tradeoffs of building reliable, scalable distributed systems.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking look at evolution from the gene's perspective, introducing the concept of the meme.
